citation: Cormier, J., & Fail Faster. (2024). LSHM: Design Your Destiny. Google Docs.
summary:
This Low Stress High Mayhem handout supports a GameRT session for Jay Cormier and Fail Faster's *Design Your Destiny: Running Out of Time*. The document describes the title as an interactive time-travel story, multiple games, and an activity book in one package. In the story, players encounter games that are broken in some way, receive guidance on how the problems might be fixed, implement their own ideas, and playtest revised versions as they help the characters through the narrative.

The handout is especially relevant as a practical artifact because it addresses teachers and librarians directly and links to resources for libraries to spread the word about *Design Your Destiny*. It also points to Jay Cormier's BoardGameGeek profile, Fail Faster's online game-design course, Cormier's other work, a Kickstarter preview actual play, and a broad set of game-design readings. These include resources on tabletop role-playing in schools and public libraries, indie game development, game jam history and practice, game jam guides, the International Conference on Game Jams, and Carnegie Mellon University Press's free *Game Jam Guide* curriculum ebook.

The game-design-tool section widens the item beyond one title. It points to Twine, RPG Maker, Unity, Godot, DIY adventure-game tools, and a one-page RPG generator. The play-alikes include *The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book*, *Cloud Dungeon*, *Honey Heist*, *Lasers and Feelings*, and Oliver Darkshire's one-page RPG template. The watch-alikes and read-alikes gather time-loop and time-travel media such as *Doctor Who*, *Russian Doll*, *Groundhog Day*, *Palm Springs*, *Slaughterhouse-Five*, *Before the Coffee Gets Cold*, *This Is How You Lose the Time War*, *A Wrinkle in Time*, *11/22/63*, and *The 7 1/2 Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle*.

The standard GameRT facilitation layer is present as well: *Script Change*, lines and veils, *The Door Is Open*, the TTRPG Safety Toolkit, Twitch policy language, GameRT links, the GameRT Zotero library, and contact information. Together these elements show a service pattern that combines a streamed game session, creator/course links, game-design pedagogy, development tools, advisory lists, and safety norms.

For the larger GBLS project, this item is useful as a primary artifact for thinking about game-design programming and library-friendly game creation resources. It suggests how a library-adjacent program can move from playing a title to discussing design repair, playtesting, game jams, tools, and curriculum resources. That practical pattern may be useful later for appendices or program templates.

This source should remain outside the main literature review. It is not scholarship, not a formal program evaluation, and not a detailed library-service case study. It may support future practical guidance on game design workshops, game jams, and advisory pathways, but it should not be cited as evidence for GBLS outcomes.
sourceText:
“Design Your Destiny” by Jay Cormier and Fail Faster
Design Your Destiny: Running Out of Time is an interactive time travel story, multiple games and an activity book all wrapped up in one package. Throughout the story you'll be asked to play a game using included components, but each game will be broken in some way. The book then provides insights and guidance on ways you could fix these problems. You'll be asked to implement your own ideas and playtest your version of these games as you try to help our main characters through their story.
Attention Teachers & Librarians!!

Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/videos/1926379610
The Game Design Your Destiny by Fail Faster
● Resources for libraries to help spread the word about Design Your Destiny ○ Poster and jpg
The Creator Jay Cormier on Board Game Geek
● Fail Faster, Jay Cormier’s Online Game Design Course ○ Online Fail Faster Game Design Program
● Jay Cormier’s Other Work ○ Off the Page Games ● MIND MGMT on BGG ● Harrow County on BGG ● Manifest Destiny on BGG
Actual Play ● DESIGN YOUR DESTINY: RUNNING OUT OF TIME | Kickstarter Preview | Fail Faster
Articles, Books, and More Info ● Let's Roll: A Guide to Setting up Tabletop Role-Playing Games in your School or Public Library ● Indie Games: From Dream to Delivery ● The Game Jam Survival Guide ● Game Jams – History, Technology, and Organisation ● Game Jam Tips ● International Conference on Game Jams, Hackathons, and Game Creation Events ● The Game Jam Guide (Free Curriculum ePub download from Carnegie Mellon UP) ● Game Maker’s Toolkit (YouTube) ● The Best Games for Learning Game Development [2020] | Ask Gamedev
Game Design Tools ● Twine ○ Twine Reference Guide ● RPG Maker

○ RPG Maker Tutorials ○ RPG Maker MZ The Basics (Sponsored by Komodo) ● Unity ● Godot ● DIY Adventure Games ● The One-Page RPG Generator | PS Berge (grave snail games)
Play Alikes ● The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book: 40 Fast, Easy, and Fun Tabletop Games ● Cloud Dungeon ● Honey Heist | Grant Howitt ● Lasers and Feelings | John Harper ● Game Template | Oliver Darkshire (a free template Oliver Darkshire created and uses for many of his one-page RPGs)
Watch Alikes Doctor Who Red Dwarf The Umbrella Academy (Netflix) Russian Doll (Netflix) Groundhog Day Palm Springs (Hulu) Before I Fall Happy Death Day
Read Alikes The Eyre Affair, by Jasper Fforde Slaughterhouse-Five, by Kurt Vonnegut Before the Coffee Gets Cold, by Toshikazu Kawaguchi This is How You Lose the Time War, by Amal El-Mohtar and Max Gladstone A Wrinkle in Time, by Madeleine L’Engle Miss Peregrine’s Home for Peculiar Children, by Ransom Riggs Oona Out of Order, by Margarita Montimore 11/22/63, by Stephen King The 7 ½ Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle, by Stuart Turton The Invisible Life of Addie LaRue, by V. E. Schwab

Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● Guest Blog: An Introduction to Lines and Veils ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive
Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Zotero: https://bit.ly/GameRTZotero Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
